member.office_address: 1107 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-1902

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
As your Representative in Congress, I can submit program funding recommendations to the Appropriations Committee of the U.S. House of Representatives that reflect the needs of our district. 

Please complete the following form to the best of your ability with as much detail as possible. Your input and participation are very helpful in determining which programs are most important to you and to our communities.

This form is for programmatic and language requests only. If you are interested in applying for Community Project Funding (CPF), please contact one of my district offices for additional information.

Thank you for your willingness to help serve our district.

Jared Golden 
Member of Congress

fields.label: Please select the most relevant appropriations committee/subcommittee to your request
fields.help_text: Select the subcommittee that has jurisdiction over your request.
options: Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education
Transportation and Housing and Urban Development
fields.rationale: NASA Science Mission Directorate funding is under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Department (Department of Transportation, Interior, Health and Human Services, etc.):
Agency (Federal Railroad Administration, National Marine Fisheries Service, etc.):
fields.rationale: NASA is the specific agency for this request.

fields.label: Account: (National Sea Grant, FINRA, etc.):
fields.rationale: The specific account within NASA is the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Indicate which of the following you are requesting (if more than one, fill out one form for each request):
fields.help_text: Programmatic requests ask for specific funding levels; Language requests ask for legislative or report text.
options: Programmatic request (for example: "Increase LIHEAP funding in President's FY 2027 budget by $10 million")
Bill language (for example: "Insert the following legislative language into the Defense Appropriations Bill...")
Report Language (for example: "Insert the following report language into the Report accompanying the Defense Appropriations Bill...")
fields.draft_value: Programmatic request (for example: "Increase LIHEAP funding in President's FY 2027 budget by $10 million")
fields.rationale: This is a request for a specific funding level ($9 billion) for an existing program.

fields.label: Please provide the bill language that you would like submitted.
fields.rationale: This is the standard format for a programmatic funding request to the CJS subcommittee, reflecting the specific $9 billion ask for the Science Mission Directorate provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Please provide the report language that you would like submitted.
fields.rationale: Combines the $9B funding target with specific ME-2 data (University of Maine, Bates College) and strategic advocacy points regarding workforce, leadership, and Decadal Survey priorities.

fields.label: How much are you requesting for your program in the FY 2027 budget?
fields.help_text: Enter the specific dollar amount requested for the upcoming fiscal year.
fields.rationale: The funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate is $9,000,000,000.

fields.label: How would your request change the current enacted level of funding for the program?
options: Support the President's FY27 budget request
fields.rationale: The request of $9 billion is a 24% increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.25 billion.

fields.label: Is this request being submitted to other Members of Congress? If so, please provide the name of each Member of Congress to whom you are submitting this budget request.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: If this request is included within a multi-Member Dear Colleague letter, please provide the name of the letter's lead Member.
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu
fields.rationale: The context identifies that an existing House letter is led by Reps. Don Bacon and Judy Chu.

top_grants.description: Validation of ocean surface downwelling irradiance and its underwater propagation for the pace mission
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Validating ocean light measurements supporting the PACE satellite.

top_grants.description: 20-biodiv20-0024 scaling forest diversity across space and time in a non-equilibrial world
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching large-scale forest diversity changes over time.

top_grants.description: Objective: tidewater glaciers have more than doubled their contribution to global sea level rise over the last two decades  with icebergs accounting for over 50% of this contribution. Historically icebergs have been linked to abrupt climate change events (E.g.  Heinrich events) and current regional models are predicting their continued role in ocean/climate impacts. However  icebergs and their freshwater flux  are not currently included in global circulation models (GCMS)  due in part to a lack of physically based iceberg parameterizations. This proposal uses remotely sensed data products  data from prior field campaigns  and an analytical model of iceberg melt to develop a predictive model of iceberg freshwater flux into the global oceans. This model will address the needs of remote sensing and iceberg melt rate studies  as well as large-scale global climate models over a range of timescales and resolutions (FIG. 1). Approach: this study will use landsat satellite imagery  in combination with >550 full-iceberg geometries and a 2d iceberg melt model  to establish parameterizations of iceberg subsurface geometry and melt rate. First  i will use existing data sets of full-iceberg geometries to compare against coincident satellite imagery to develop an empirical relationship between subsurface iceberg shape and its surface expression. Then i will use the full-iceberg geometries to establish representative iceberg geometries based on size and geographic location. Next  i will use these representative geometries to update a current iceberg melt model and calculate 4- dimensional freshwater flux. I will validate the melt rate parameterization using direct observations of iceberg melt collected during 2017-2019 field campaigns. Finally  i will combine the above parameterizations to establish a predictive model of iceberg freshwater flux based upon an iceberg S surface expression and location. Uniqueness: previous remote sensing studies of icebergs have focused on freshwater flux in specific fjords using only a subset of available satellite data  preferentially selecting large  easily identifiable  icebergs. This proposal leverages in situ iceberg data (1973  2019) spanning a range of iceberg sizes and shapes to develop a single iceberg parameterization that can be applied globally within ocean models and gcms to represent and predict freshwater flux. Relevance to NASA: this project seeks to understand iceberg geometry and deterioration both physically  at the individual iceberg level  and statistically  at the global ocean scale. The proposed work directly addresses the interagency arctic research policy committee S goals by develop[ing] data sets to improve global reanalysis systems   in the arctic  and  enhance understanding of complex relationships between ocean  sea ice  land and atmosphere[.]  further  this large-scale effort directly addresses both the  water cycle  and  Earth surface  of the NASA nip. This project leverages insight on full-iceberg geometries  the physical processes of iceberg deterioration  and satellite-derived iceberg data to constrain global iceberg metrics. It expands the community S knowledge by taking the first step toward creating a series of iceberg geometries and a freshwater flux module for regular use in gcms and global ocean circulation models to promote better decisions about our environment and water.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Predicting iceberg freshwater contributions to global sea level rise models.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Advancing strategic science for Moon, Mars exploration, and planetary defense.

top_grants.recipient_name: President and Trustees of Bates College
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Researching the physics of matter in extremely cold quantum states.

top_grant_recipients.name: President and Trustees of Bates College
